heading: Tribes excepted from certain provisions
label: 339 Tribes excepted from certain provisions
text: The provisions of this act shall not extend to the territory occupied by the Cherokees, Creeks, Choctaws, Chickasaws, Seminoles, and Osage, Miamies and Peorias, and Sacs and Foxes, in Oklahoma, nor to any of the reservations of the Seneca Nation of New York Indians in the State of New York, nor to that strip of territory in the State of Nebraska adjoining the Sioux Nation on the south added by Executive order.
notes: References in TextThis act, referred to in text, is , , and is popularly known as the Indian General Allotment Act. For classification of this act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act Feb. 8, 1887, ch. 11924 Stat. 388section 331 of this title

Sacs and Foxes; Missouri IndiansNo allotment of lands was to be made or annuities of money to be paid to any of the Sacs and Foxes of the Missouri Indians who were not enrolled as members of the tribe on , by a proviso annexed to , .Jan. 1, 1890act Feb. 28, 1891, ch. 383, § 526 Stat. 796
